Powered by Entire:
  This conversation will be linked to your next commit.

- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- playwright-cli: Automates browser interactions for web testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction. Use when the user needs to navigate websites, interact with web pages, fill forms, take screenshots, test web applications, or extract information from web pages.
- reviewing-code: ベースブランチに対する変更差分をもとにコードレビューを実施します。ベースブランチを指示として含めることができます。含めない場合は main をベースブランチとしてレビューを行います。レビューを依頼されたら必ず使用する
- pr: GitHub の Pull Request を自動作成するスキル。 「PR を作って」「プルリクを作成して」「PR を出して」「レビュー依頼したい」など、 PR 作成・提出に関する指示があれば必ずこのスキルを使用すること。 GitHub Issues からチケットの内容を取得し、git log でベースブランチを特定、 git diff からコンテキストを把握した上で、所定のテンプレートを使って develop ブランチへの PR を作成する。
- searching-library-reference: Context7 API を利用して外部ライブラリのAPIドキュメントとコードスニペットを検索・取得します。外部ライブラリの使用方法を調べる際に必ず使用します。
- commit: git のステージの内容を元にコミットを行います
- supabase:supabase-postgres-best-practices: Postgres performance optimization and best practices from Supabase. Use this skill when writing, reviewing, or optimizing Postgres queries, schema designs, or database configurations.
- supabase:supabase: Use when doing ANY task involving Supabase. Triggers: Supabase products (Database, Auth, Edge Functions, Realtime, Storage, Vectors, Cron, Queues); client libraries and SSR integrations (supabase-js, @supabase/ssr) in Next.js, React, SvelteKit, Astro, Remix; auth issues (login, logout, sessions, JWT, cookies, getSession, getUser, getClaims, RLS); Supabase CLI or MCP server; schema changes, migrations, security audits, Postgres extensions (pg_graphql, pg_cron, pg_vector).
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt: What changed in v2.0.0? Is "version: latest" still supported? What are the breaking changes for the version input?

content.content:
# v2.0.0 Changes & Version Input

## What Changed

The release "refreshes the action internals, CI coverage, and release pipeline while keeping usage straightforward with `uses: supabase/setup-cli@v2`."

Key improvements include:
- **Composite action implementation** with modernized runtime and dependencies
- **Smarter version detection**: When omitted, the action now checks root lockfiles (`bun.lock`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `package-lock.json`) before defaulting to latest
- **Enhanced validation** through dedicated CI and E2E workflows
- **Stronger security** with pinned actions and hardened supply-chain practices

## Version Input Support

Yes, `version: latest` remains supported. The release notes indicate that when the version parameter is omitted, it falls back to `latest` as a default option.

## Breaking Changes

The documentation doesn't explicitly list breaking changes. However, the major version bump (v1.6.0 → v2.0.0) suggests internal architectural shifts. Users should review updated docs for the `@v2` tag, though the stated goal was maintaining "straightforward" usage.

input.prompt: How is "latest" version resolved? Is there an HTTP call that could return 404 when version is "latest"?

content.content:
# How "latest" Version is Resolved

Based on the code, when version is set to "latest", the `getDownloadUrl()` function constructs this URL:

```
https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/latest/download/{filename}
```

Yes, there is potential for a 404 error. GitHub's `/releases/latest/` endpoint could theoretically return 404 if no releases exist, though this is unlikely for an active project. The code downloads the tarball via `tc.downloadTool()`, which would fail if the URL returns a 404 status.

The function doesn't include any retry logic or fallback mechanism to handle such HTTP errors—it would simply propagate the failure to the catch block in the `run()` function.

input.prompt: Show me the exact filename pattern used in the download URL. What does {filename} look like? Show the platform/arch detection logic.

content.content:
# Download URL Filename Pattern

The filename pattern is: **`supabase_{platform}_{arch}.tar.gz`**

## Platform/Arch Detection Logic

The code maps system values to archive-compatible names:

**Platform mapping:**
- `"win32"` → `"windows"`
- All other platforms → unchanged (e.g., `"linux"`, `"darwin"`)

**Architecture mapping:**
- `"x64"` → `"amd64"`
- All other architectures → unchanged (e.g., `"arm64"`)

The functions `getArchivePlatform()` and `getArchiveArch()` perform these conversions using the Node.js `process.platform` and `process.arch` values.

## URL Construction

For versions after `1.28.0`, the simplified pattern applies:
```
https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/download/v{version}/supabase_{platform}_{arch}.tar.gz
```

For earlier versions, the version string is embedded differently in the filename path.

2. Checks `GITHUB_WORKSPACE` environment variable for lockfile detection
3. Falls back to `"latest"` as the default

## URL Construction Logic

The `getDownloadUrl()` function handles two distinct URL patterns:

**For "latest" versions:**
```
https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/latest/download/supabase_{platform}_{arch}.tar.gz
```

**For versions before 1.28.0:**
```
https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/download/v{version}/supabase_{version}_{platform}_{arch}.tar.gz
```

**For versions 1.28.0 and later:**
```
https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/download/v{version}/supabase_{platform}_{arch}.tar.gz
```

The constant `REGISTRY_VERSION = "1.28.0"` serves as the threshold. The code uses `semver.order()` to determine which URL pattern applies—returning `-1` for older versions, `0` or `1` for newer ones.

Platform and architecture values are normalized (e.g., `x64` → `amd64`, `win32` → `windows`) before URL construction.

content.content:
# getDownloadUrl Function Analysis

The `getDownloadUrl` function constructs download URLs for the Supabase CLI based on the requested version:

**For "latest":**
```
https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/latest/download/{filename}
```
where `{filename}` = `supabase_{platform}_{arch}.tar.gz`

**For versions < 1.28.0:**
```
https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/download/v{version}/supabase_{version}_{platform}_{arch}.tar.gz
```
The version string is embedded in both the path and filename.

**For versions ≥ 1.28.0:**
```
https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/download/v{version}/{filename}
```
The version appears only in the path; the filename follows the simplified `supabase_{platform}_{arch}.tar.gz` pattern.

The function also maps platform names (e.g., `win32` → `windows`) and architecture names (e.g., `x64` → `amd64`) to match GitHub release naming conventions.

aiTitle: CI エラーの調査と修正

content.text:
原因が判明したので、ユーザーへ報告します。

---

## CI 失敗の原因

PR #263 の CI(`静的解析` / `Server Test` / `E2E Test` / `Claude Code Review`)が全て同じ「Supabase CLI のセットアップ」ステップで **`Unexpected HTTP response: 404`** で落ちています。

### 根本原因(PR 由来ではなく upstream の不整合)

`supabase/setup-cli@v2.0.0` の URL 解決ロジックと `supabase/cli` リリース側のアセット命名が噛み合っていません。

- **setup-cli@v2.0.0 が期待する URL**
  `https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/latest/download/supabase_linux_amd64.tar.gz`
  (バージョン番号なしのファイル名)
- **supabase/cli の最新リリース v2.99.0(本日 09:29 公開)に実在するアセット**
  `supabase_2.99.0_linux_amd64.tar.gz`(バージョン番号入り)

実際に curl で叩くと:
```
HTTP/2 404
```
`version: latest` のままだと、アセット名が一致せず取得不能です。

### この PR で「壊れた」わけではない点

- `main` ブランチも同じ `supabase/setup-cli@v2` + `version: latest` を使用
- `main` の最後の成功 CI は `2026-05-17 22:13Z`、`supabase/cli v2.99.0` の公開は本日 `2026-05-18 09:29` → タイミング的に upstream リリースで踏み抜いた状態
- つまり main を流し直しても同じく落ちます

### 対処案(どちらでも復旧可)

1. **`version` を明示的に固定**(推奨・小さい変更)
   `.github/workflows/*.yml` の `supabase/setup-cli` の `with:` で
   ```yaml
   with:
     version: 2.99.0
   ```
   とする。ただし setup-cli v2 は `>=1.28.0` の場合 `supabase_{platform}_{arch}.tar.gz` を期待するロジックなので、これでも 404 になる可能性が高い。実際にはバージョン入りファイル名を期待する setup-cli v1 系か、別の入手手段が必要。
2. **`supabase/setup-cli@v1.6.0` の SHA(`b60b5899c73b63a2d2d651b1e90db8d4c9392f51`)に降格 + `version: 2.98.x` などを固定**
   v1 の挙動を見ても結局 `>=1.28.0` で同じ URL を組むので、これも本質的に救えません。
3. **`pnpm-lock.yaml` に `supabase` パッケージを追加して `version:` 入力を外す**
   v2.0.0 で導入された「lockfile からバージョン検出」を使う方法。lockfile に `supabase@<x.y.z>` があれば、それを使うため latest 解決の 404 を回避できる可能性があります(ただし結局 setup-cli の URL 構築バグを踏むなら同じ)。
4. **`supabase/setup-cli` を諦め、ワークフローで直接ダウンロード**
   ```yaml
   - run: |
       curl -fsSL https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/download/v2.99.0/supabase_2.99.0_linux_amd64.tar.gz \
         | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin supabase
   ```
   最も確実ですが変更が大きめ。

### 次に取るべきアクション

`supabase/setup-cli` の Issue Tracker を確認するのが最短です(upstream で既に報告されている可能性が高い)。私の側で着手する場合、上記 4 案の中で**(3)→(4) の順**で試すのが筋が良いと考えます。どの方針で進めますか?

# Issue Summary

**Issue:** `latest` throws 404 for a few minutes after a new release (#115)

**Description:** When using `version: latest` in the setup-cli, a 404 error is thrown temporarily following a new release.

**Expected Behavior:** The `latest` version tag should resolve successfully to the most recent release without throwing errors.

**Status:** Closed (no additional details provided in the content)

**Conclusion/Workaround:** The issue content does not include a resolution, workaround, or linked pull request. The bug appears to stem from a timing issue where the `latest` tag isn't immediately available after deployment, suggesting a synchronization delay in the release infrastructure.

**Note:** The issue lacks closure information, linked PRs, or documented solutions in the provided content.

input.prompt: Summarize the issue, what people reported, and the resolution including any linked PR. Was it about asset naming or 404?

content.content:
# Summary of GitHub Issue #410

## The Issue
Users reported an intermittent failure with `supabase/setup-cli` v2 where the action would complete successfully but the `supabase` command would be unavailable in the next workflow step. The environment variable `SUPABASE_INTERNAL_IMAGE_REGISTRY` was also missing.

## Root Cause
The reporter identified a potential race condition in the code's async entrypoint. The relevant code shows:

> "if (process.argv[1] === fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)) { void run(); }"

The `run()` function performs critical tasks like `core.addPath(cliPath)` and `core.exportVariable()`, but since it's asynchronous and wasn't being awaited, the action could exit before these side effects persisted to subsequent workflow steps.

## Resolution
The issue was linked to PR #411, indicating the maintainers addressed the race condition—likely by properly awaiting the async `run()` function to ensure all setup operations completed before the action finished.

Users reported an intermittent failure with `supabase/setup-cli` v2 where the action would complete successfully but the `supabase` command would be unavailable in the next workflow step. The environment variable `SUPABASE_INTERNAL_IMAGE_REGISTRY` was also missing.

## Root Cause
The reporter identified a potential race condition in the code's async entrypoint. The relevant code shows:

> "if (process.argv[1] === fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)) { void run(); }"

The `run()` function performs critical tasks like `core.addPath(cliPath)` and `core.exportVariable()`, but since it's asynchronous and wasn't being awaited, the action could exit before these side effects persisted to subsequent workflow steps.

## Resolution
The issue was linked to PR #411, indicating the maintainers addressed the race condition—likely by properly awaiting the async `run()` function to ensure all setup operations completed before the action finished.

# GitHub Issue Summary: Supabase CLI v2.99.0 Release Asset Naming

## Issue Overview
The latest Supabase CLI release (v2.99.0) changed how release assets are named, breaking the `supabase/setup-cli` GitHub Action when using `version: latest`.

## Root Cause
Asset naming convention changed inconsistently:
- **Previous versions (e.g., 2.98.2)**: `supabase_linux_amd64.tar.gz` (no version in filename)
- **v2.99.0**: `supabase_2.99.0_linux_amd64.tar.gz` (version included in filename)

The GitHub Action code expects the older naming format and cannot locate the new asset files, resulting in 404 errors.

## Status
**Closed as duplicate** of issue #5257, indicating this was a known problem with an existing tracked solution.

## Recommended Workaround
Specify a fixed CLI version instead of using `latest` until the underlying naming inconsistency is resolved:
```
version: 2.98.2  # or another compatible version
```

## Resolution
The issue points to code in the setup-cli repository that needs updating to handle the new asset naming convention or the release process needs standardization.

# Issue Summary

**Issue**: The Supabase CLI GitHub Action fails when using the `latest` release tag because version 2.99.0 is missing binary artifacts.

**Root Cause**: Version 2.99.0 lacks the expected build artifacts (like `supabase_linux_amd64.tar.gz`) that the action attempts to download. Previous versions like 2.98.1 included these files.

**Status**: Open (no resolution provided in the thread)

**Error Message**: "Failed to download from `https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/latest/download/supabase_linux_amd64.tar.gz`. Code(404) Message(Not Found)"

**Workaround**: Pin a specific working version (2.98.1 or earlier) in your GitHub Action configuration rather than using `latest`.

**Key Detail**: The issue shows that 2.99.0 release artifacts are incomplete compared to 2.98.1, causing automated workflows relying on the latest tag to break unexpectedly.

## Issue Tracker 調査結果

**upstream で本日報告済み・対応待ちの障害**でした。

### 該当 issue

- **[supabase/cli#5257](https://github.com/supabase/cli/issues/5257)**「v2.99.0 - Supabase CLI action started failing because package is missing」
  - 状態: **Open**(created 2026-05-18 09:52Z、本日)
  - 内容: v2.99.0 のリリースで goreleaser のアセット命名規則が変わり、`supabase_linux_amd64.tar.gz`(バージョン番号なし)が消滅。setup-cli はこのファイル名で取りに行くため 404。
  - 別ユーザーからも追認コメントあり
- **[supabase/cli#5259](https://github.com/supabase/cli/issues/5259)**「Newest 2.99.0 releases lead to 404 error when using `supabase/setup-cli` ... `latest`」
  - 状態: Closed(#5257 の duplicate)

### 確定した事実

- 当 PR のコード差分(SHA ピン留め)は**無関係**。原因は upstream のリリース問題。
- `main` ブランチで再実行しても同じく落ちる(同じ workflow / 同じ `version: latest`)。

### 公式ワークアラウンド

`supabase/cli` メンテナの正式な fix は出ておらず、ユーザー側は `version` を **`2.98.2`**(v2.99.0 直前の最後の正常リリース)に固定するのが現時点の唯一の手段です:

```yaml
- name: Supabase CLI のセットアップ
  uses: supabase/setup-cli@df56b21da46c98abb12a9804e4fb1f657773e333 # v2.0.0
  with:
    version: 2.98.2
```

対象は当 PR で触っている 4 ファイル(`static-analytics.yml` / `server-test.yml` / `e2e-test.yml` / `browser-test.yml`)。upstream 修復後は `latest` に戻して差し支えありません。

この対応(`2.98.2` 固定)を PR #263 に追加する形で進めますか?それとも upstream の修正を待ちますか?

# GitHub Action 実装ガイドライン

GitHub Actions ワークフローは以下のルールに従うこと。

## 必須設定

### ステップ名の日本語化
各ステップには日本語でわかりやすい名前を必ず付けること。

例:
- `name: リポジトリのチェックアウト`
- `name: 依存関係のインストール`

### pnpm バージョン
`pnpm/action-setup` でバージョンを省略し、`package.json` の `packageManager` フィールドのバージョンを自動使用させること。

- ❌ 悪い例: `version: 10.12.4`
- ✅ 良い例: バージョン指定なし（省略）

### Node.js バージョン
`setup-node` で `.node-version` ファイルを参照すること。

```yaml
- uses: actions/setup-node@v4
  with:
    node-version-file: .node-version
```

### concurrency 設定
重複実行を防ぐため、原則的に concurrency を設定すること。設定しない場合はワークフローファイルにその理由をコメントで残すこと。

```yaml
concurrency:
  group: <workflow-name>-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
```

### タイムアウト設定
各ジョブには最低でも 10 分のタイムアウトを設定すること。

```yaml
jobs:
  build:
    timeout-minutes: 10
```

## 実装例

```yaml
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - name: リポジトリのチェックアウト
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: pnpm のセットアップ
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        # version は省略（package.json の packageManager を使用）

      - name: Node.js のセットアップ
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: .node-version
          cache: pnpm

      - name: 依存関係のインストール
        run: pnpm install

      - name: ビルド
        run: pnpm -w build
```

name: E2E Test

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

concurrency:
  group: e2e-test-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  APP_NAME: FORMAWORK.AI
  SERVICE_NAME: web
  SUPABASE_URL: http://127.0.0.1:62021
  SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: REDACTED
  SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: REDACTED
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: http://127.0.0.1:62021
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: REDACTED
  DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@127.0.0.1:62022/postgres

jobs:
  medium-e2e-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15

    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        # browser: [chromium, webkit]
        browser: [chromium]

    steps:
      - name: リポジトリのチェックアウト
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - name: pnpm のセットアップ
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@0e279bb959325dab635dd2c09392533439d90093 # v6.0.8

      - name: Node.js のセットアップ
        uses: actions/setup-node@48b55a011bda9f5d6aeb4c2d9c7362e8dae4041e # v6.4.0
        with:
          node-version-file: .node-version
          cache: pnpm

      - name: 依存関係のインストール
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Playwright ブラウザのインストール
        # userSetupProject で chromium を使うため常に chromium はインストール
        run: pnpm --filter web exec playwright install --with-deps chromium ${{ matrix.browser }}

      - name: Supabase CLI のセットアップ
        uses: supabase/setup-cli@df56b21da46c98abb12a9804e4fb1f657773e333 # v2.0.0
        with:
          version: latest

      - name: Supabase の起動
        run: |
          pnpm -w supabase:start && pnpm -w supabase:seed
      - name: データベースリセット
        run: pnpm -w db:reset
      - name: Next.js のビルド
        run: pnpm --filter web build

      - name: E2E テストの実行
        run: pnpm -w test:e2e --project=${{ matrix.browser }}

      - name: テストレポートのアップロード
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@043fb46d1a93c77aae656e7c1c64a875d1fc6a0a # v7.0.1
        with:
          name: playwright-report-${{ matrix.browser }}
          path: apps/web/playwright-report/
          retention-days: 7

      - name: テスト結果アップロード
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@043fb46d1a93c77aae656e7c1c64a875d1fc6a0a # v7.0.1
        with:
          name: playwright-screenshots-${{ matrix.browser }}
          path: apps/web/test-results/
          retention-days: 7
